description: 'ruka' applies to an arrangement with many parts, steps, or relations whose connections matter. A route may be complex because its decisions interact, and a concept because several claims depend on one another. 'siloma' (simple) has few relations to follow; 'kethua' (hard) concerns the effort a task demands, so complexity and difficulty remain separate judgements.
articulatory_notes: Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip for 'r', then raise the back of the tongue into stressed high rounded 'u'. Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release it into open central unrounded 'a'.
sound_symbolism: A brief tongue contact at the front of the mouth gives way to a full closure at the back. Two distinct points fit inside this compact word, much as several parts may fit inside one complex arrangement.
usage_notes: Noun use supplies complexity. When English complicated means both many-parted and difficult, Phi can state 'ruka' and 'kethua' as two qualities instead of asking one adjective to carry both.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: An Art Nouveau grille may be 'ruka' because its stems and curves form a pattern with many relations. Complexity describes the arrangement before anyone praises it.

semantic_domains.cognition: an idea, explanation, or route with many relations to follow
semantic_domains.creation: a designed object or system with interacting parts
semantic_domains.nature: a living or ecological system with many interacting relations
